DIA_PLAS_C_BREAST_01_V2.name: Breast Augmentation (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_BREAST_01_V2.description: Breast Augmentation, also known as augmentation mammoplasty, can be a cosmetic (C) procedure or a reconstructive (R) procedure in the case of breast reconstruction surgery. The procedure involves implants being inserted beneath the breasts to enlarge them. The implant type and size depends on factors such as how much bigger the patient wants their breasts to be, their breast anatomy, skin thickness and elasticity, and body type. The patient has expressed that they want a Cosmetic Breast Augmentation to enhance their appearance.

DIA_PLAS_C_BREAST_02.name: Breast Lift (Mastopexy)C)
DIA_PLAS_C_BREAST_02.description: Breast lift, also known as mastopexy, is a procedure that raises and firms the breasts. The surgical procedure also reshapes the nipple-areola complex and lifts the breasts on the chest wall. The patient has expressed that they want a Breast Lift to enhance their appearance.

DIA_PLAS_C_NOSE_01_V2.name: Augmentation Rhinoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_NOSE_01_V2.description: Augmentation Rhinoplasty is surgery that changes the shape of the nose. The motivation for rhinoplasty may be to change the appearance of the nose, improve breathing or both. Augmentation Rhinoplasty is commonly referred to as a “nose job". This surgical procedure works to enhance the outward look of the nose, or its external aesthetics. This often involves reshaping a “crooked” nose or bringing a large nose into better proportion with the rest of the face.

DIA_PLAS_C_NOSE_02.name: Septoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_NOSE_02.description: Septoplasty, or septal reconstruction, is a corrective surgical procedure done to straighten a deviated nasal septum – the nasal septum being the partition between the two nasal cavities. Ideally, the septum should run down the centre of the nose. The patient has expressed that they want a Septoplasty to enhance their appearance.

DIA_PLAS_C_STOMACH_01_V2.name: Liposuction (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_STOMACH_01_V2.description: Liposuction, also known as lipectomy or lipoplasty, is a procedure that improves the body’s contours and proportion by removing excess fat deposits to slim and reshape specific areas of the body. Common areas targeted include thighs, buttocks, abdomen, arms, neck and under the chin. Usually liposuction of smaller volumes are done as an outpatient while larger volumes require a stay in hospital to monitor fluid levels and if patients are suffering from any other medical conditions. The patient has expressed that they want a Liposuction to enhance their appearance.

DIA_PLAS_C_HAIR_01_V2.name: Hair Transplant (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_HAIR_01_V2.description: Hair transplantation is a surgical technique that removes hair follicles from one part of the body, called the 'donor site', to a bald or balding part of the body known as the 'recipient site'. The technique is primarily used to treat male pattern baldness. In this minimally invasive procedure, grafts containing hair follicles that are genetically resistant to balding (like the back of the head) are transplanted to the bald scalp. Hair transplantation can also be used to restore eyelashes, eyebrows, beard hair, chest hair, pubic hair and to fill in scars caused by accidents or surgery such as face-lifts and previous hair transplants.

DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_02.name: Skin Conditioning (Botulinum Toxin Therapy)(C)
DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_02.description: Botox injections are noted primarily for the ability to reduce the appearance of facial wrinkles. Botox injections block certain chemical signals from nerves, mostly signals that cause muscles to contract. The most common use of these injections is to temporarily relax the facial muscles that cause wrinkles in the forehead and around the eyes.

DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_03.name: Skin Conditioning (Dermal Fillers)(C)
DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_03.description: Dermal fillers, sometimes called soft tissue fillers, are materials injected under the skin to reduce the appearance of facial wrinkles or lines, and can also be used to enhance facial contours by adding volume to cheeks and lips and to improve the appearance of scars. Dermal filler products are biodegradable, which means they will naturally disappear in the body. The longevity of the filler can vary, depending on the brand, thickness and area treated.

DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_04.name: Skin Rejuvenation (Laser Resurfacing)(C)
DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_04.description: Laser resurfacing is a facial rejuvenation procedure that uses a laser to improve the skin's appearance or treat minor facial flaws. Laser resurfacing can decrease the appearance of fine lines in the face. It can also treat loss of skin tone and improve the patient's complexion if they have scars or sun damage. Laser resurfacing can't eliminate excessive or sagging skin.

DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_01.name: Chemical Peel (Light Peel)(C)
DIA_PLAS_C_SKIN_01.description: Chemical peel is a form of treatment that uses chemical solutions to correct facial blemishes, wrinkles and uneven skin pigmentation. In this treatment, a chemical solution is applied to the skin, which makes it "blister" and eventually peel off. The new skin is usually smoother and less wrinkled than the old skin.

DIA_PLAS_C_VEIN_01.name: Telangiectasias (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_VEIN_01.description: Telangiectasias, commonly know as Spider Veins are clusters of tiny blood vessels that develop close to the surface of the skin. They are often red, blue, or purple; and they have the appearance of a spiderweb. They are commonly found on the face and legs.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHEEK_01.name: Cheek Augmentation (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHEEK_01.description: Cheek augmentation, also sometimes called cheek implants, are inserted to enhance cheekbones or to make the cheeks fuller. Like other types of plastic surgery procedures, cheek augmentation can make the patient's face appear more youthful or balance their facial features.

DIA_PLAS_C_TUMMY_01.name: Abdominoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_TUMMY_01.description: Abdominoplasty or "tummy tuck" is a cosmetic surgery procedure used to make the abdomen thinner and more firm. The surgery involves the removal of excess skin and fat from the middle and lower abdomen in order to tighten the muscle and fascia of the abdominal wall.

DIA_PLAS_R_NOSE_01.name: Nasal Deformities
DIA_PLAS_R_NOSE_01.description: The patient's nose has structural defects that are causing issues both aesthetically and functionally.

DIA_PLAS_R_EYE_01.name: Blepharospasm (R)(Mild)
DIA_PLAS_R_EYE_01.description: Blepharospasm is abnormal contraction of the eyelid muscles. It often refers to benign essential blepharospasm (BEB) which is a bilateral condition and a form of focal dystonia leading to episodic closure of the eyelids. In more mild cases, Botulinum Toxin injections is a commonly accepted treatment. It takes between one and four days for the paralysis to begin. The complete effect usually takes about a week and often lasts up to four months.

DIA_PLAS_R_EYE_02_V2.name: Strabismus (R)
DIA_PLAS_R_EYE_02_V2.description: Strabismus is a condition in which the eyes do not properly align with each other when looking at an object. The eye that is focused on an object can alternate. The condition may be present occasionally or constantly. If present during a large part of childhood, it may result in amblyopia or loss of depth perception. Although it is often considered a childhood condition, the incidence of strabismus is actually higher in adults.

DIA_PLAS_R_HAND_01_M.name: Dupuytren’s Contracture (Simple)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_HAND_01_M.description: Dupuytren contracture, also know as palmar fibromatosis, is characterised by a deformity of the hand in which the joints of one or more fingers cannot be fully straightened, it usually develops over years. The condition affects a layer of tissue that lies under the skin of your palm. Knots of tissue form under the skin — eventually creating a thick cord that can pull one or more fingers into a bent position. When simple, the condition can be relatively easy to correct without needing surgical intervention.

DIA_PLAS_R_HAND_01_F.name: Dupuytren’s Contracture (Simple)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_HAND_01_F.description: Dupuytren contracture, also know as palmar fibromatosis, is characterised by a deformity of the hand in which the joints of one or more fingers cannot be fully straightened, it usually develops over years. The condition affects a layer of tissue that lies under the skin of your palm. Knots of tissue form under the skin — eventually creating a thick cord that can pull one or more fingers into a bent position. When simple, the condition can be relatively easy to correct without needing surgical intervention.

DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_01.name: Varicose Veins (Simple)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_01.description: Varicose veins are twisted, enlarged veins. Any superficial vein may become varicosed, but the veins most commonly affected are those in the legs. For many patients, varicose veins are simply a cosmetic concern. For other patients, varicose veins can cause aching pain and discomfort and may lead to more-serious problems.

DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_02_V2.name: Varicose Veins (Complex)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_02_V2.description: Varicose veins are twisted, enlarged veins. Any superficial vein may become varicosed, but the veins most commonly affected are those in the legs. For many patients, varicose veins are simply a cosmetic concern. For other patients, varicose veins can cause aching pain and discomfort and may lead to more-serious problems.

DIA_PLAS_R_SCAR_01.name: Hypertrophic Scar (Simple)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_SCAR_01.description: A hypertrophic scar is a thickened, wide, often raised scar that develops where skin is injured. Scars are common during the wound healing process, but a hypertrophic scar is a result of an abnormal response to a trauma or injury. The scars are a frequent complication of burn injuries, but can also form after piercings, cuts, or even acne. Hypertrophic scars are similar to keloid scars but tend to be milder and don’t grow beyond the boundaries of the original skin injury. The scars aren’t dangerous or life-threatening. They can be itchy and painful, but more often are simply a cosmetic issue.

DIA_PLAS_R_SCAR_02.name: Keloid Scar (Simple)(R)
DIA_PLAS_R_SCAR_02.description: Keloids are a type of raised scar. They occur where the skin has healed after an injury. They can grow to be much larger than the original injury that caused the scar. Anything that can cause a scar can cause a keloid. This includes being burned, cut, or having severe acne.

DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_03.name: Venous Ulcer (R)
DIA_PLAS_R_VEIN_03.description: Venous ulcers, also referred to as stasis, insufficiency or varicose ulcers, are the result of malfunctioning venous valves causing pressure in the veins to increase. These typically occur along the medial or lateral distal (lower) leg. The resulting venous hypertension causes blood pooling when it is not as efficiently pumped back towards the heart, otherwise known as venous insufficiency. Eventual breakdown of tissues due to lack of oxygen and nutrients, specifically, deposits around the capillaries of the protein fibrin, which normally plays a role in clotting, prevent oxygen and nutrients from flowing to the surrounding muscle and tissue and in turn lead to necrosis and ulceration (Fibrin Cuff Theory).

DIA_PLAS_R_T_REPLANT_01_V2.name: Amputated Digit (GT)(R)(HB)
DIA_PLAS_R_T_REPLANT_01_V2.description: Traumatic amputation is the loss of a body part that occurs as the result of an accident or injury. In this case, a digit on the patient's hand has been amputated and requires immediate medical attention to attempt to restore function to the hand and digit. Sharp, guillotine-type (GT) injuries with relatively uninjured surrounding tissue have the best post-replantation prognosis, with a success rate of 77%. Severe crush injuries (CI), multi-level injuries, and avulsion injuries often mangle soft tissue to the point of precluding rejoining of essential blood vessels, making replantation impossible. In such cases, revision amputation of the stump may be necessary. The patient has arrived to the hospital with the severed body part (HB).

DIA_PLAS_R_T_REPLANT_02.name: Amputated Toe (GT)(R)(HB)
DIA_PLAS_R_T_REPLANT_02.description: Traumatic amputation is the loss of a body part that occurs as the result of an accident or injury. In this case, a toe on the patient's foot has been amputated and requires immediate medical attention to attempt to restore function to the foot and toe. Sharp, guillotine-type (GT) injuries with relatively uninjured surrounding tissue have the best post-replantation prognosis, with a success rate of 77%. Severe crush injuries (CI), multi-level injuries, and avulsion injuries often mangle soft tissue to the point of precluding rejoining of essential blood vessels, making replantation impossible. In such cases, revision amputation of the stump may be necessary. The patient has arrived to the hospital with the severed body part (HB).

DIA_PLAS_R_T_BLAST_01.name: Blast-Related Ocular Trauma
DIA_PLAS_R_T_BLAST_01.description: Blast-related ocular trauma comprises a specialised group of penetrating and blunt force injuries to the eye and its structure caused by the detonation of explosive materials. The patients eye is all but obliterated making recovery impossible and the situation critical. Emergency care and surgery will be required to prevent any further blood loss, infections and damage to surrounding tissue.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_01.name: Checkup: Breast Augmentation (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_01.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Breast Augmentation surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_02.name: Checkup: Augmentation Rhinoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_02.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Augmentation Rhinoplasty surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_03.name: Checkup: Liposuction (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_03.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Liposuction surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_04.name: Checkup: Mastopexy (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_04.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Mastopexy surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_05.name: Checkup: Septoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_05.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Septoplasty surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_06.name: Checkup: Cheek Augmentation (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_06.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Cheek Augmentation surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_07.name: Checkup: Abdominoplasty (C)
DIA_PLAS_C_CHECKUP_07.description: The patient has a follow up appointment with their doctor. The patient is following up a recent Abdominoplasty surgery and the doctor will check the wound, replace any dressings and remove any stitches, staples, adhesives or drains that are still in place if necessary.

SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.name: Nodule On The Skin
SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.description: A small, shiny bump or nodule on the skin

SYM_CRP_M_SCAR_TISSUE.name: Minor Scar Tissue
SYM_CRP_M_SCAR_TISSUE.description: A scar is a mark that is left on the skin after a wound or an injury to the surface of the skin has healed. The formation of scars or scar tissue is a normal part of the healing process, and occurs when the skin is cut due to injury or surgery. The growth of scar tissue is influenced by many factors, these include: The severity of the original injury. The size, depth and location of the wound on the body.

SYM_CRP_H_BENT_DIGIT.name: Bent Digit Of The Hand
SYM_CRP_H_BENT_DIGIT.description: The patient has presented with a finger that is bent inwards on their hand and while they still have motor control of the finger the patient is unable to correct the bending themselves.

SYM_CRP_H_THICKCORD.name: Prominent Cord
SYM_CRP_H_THICKCORD.description: The patient has a hardened and thick muscle/tissue cord on their hand.

SYM_CRP_THPAFA.name: Thickening Of The Palmar Fascia
SYM_CRP_THPAFA.description: The palmar fascia is a triangular structure in the palm of the hand based distally. When thickened it becomes tight and shortened, contracting the fingers inwards toward the palm.

SYM_CRP_DIMPLES.name: Dimpling Of The Skin
SYM_CRP_DIMPLES.description: The patient's skin has a dimpled appearance, similar to the surface structure of an orange peel or golf ball.

SYM_CRP_OOST.name: Overgrowth Of Scar Tissue
SYM_CRP_OOST.description: Overgrowth of scar tissue occurs when too much collagen is produced at the site of the wound. The scar keeps growing, even after the wound has healed.

SYM_CRP_BLEEDING2.name: Light Bleeding
SYM_CRP_BLEEDING2.description: The patient is bleeding lightly, there is typically no change in vital signs and fluid resuscitation is not usually necessary. Stopping the bleeding may be necessary to prevent further complications.

SYM_CRP_BLEEDING3.name: Moderate Bleeding
SYM_CRP_BLEEDING3.description: The patient is bleeding somewhat heavily, invention is necessary to prevent hypovolemic shock.

SYM_CRP_RAPID_BLINKING.name: Excessive Blinking
SYM_CRP_RAPID_BLINKING.description: Blinking is a reflex, which means your body does it automatically. You can also make yourself blink when you want to. Excessive blinking is when you blink more than you want to. Excessive blinking is caused by over-stimulation of the blinking reflex. This is most commonly due to a foreign body in the eye. This can be as simple as an ingrown eyelash rubbing against the front of the eyeball. Thus anything which causes a corneal abrasion causes excessive blinking and watering in an attempt to wash out the foreign body. Excessive blinking may also be caused by allergic reactions causing watering, itching and roughening of the conjunctiva. There are other also many other disease states associated with excessive blinking. Treatment for excessive blinking depends on the underlying cause.

SYM_CRP_LAZY_EYE.name: Amblyopia (Lazy Eye)
SYM_CRP_LAZY_EYE.description: Amblyopia is a vision development disorder in which an eye fails to achieve normal visual acuity, even with prescription eyeglasses or contact lenses. Also called lazy eye, amblyopia begins during infancy and early childhood. In most cases, only one eye is affected.

SYM_SLP_FAILED_BA.name: Failed Breast Augmentation
SYM_SLP_FAILED_BA.description: Due to surgery complications, the patient has been unable to have a successful cosmetic surgery outcome. They will not be happy about this.

SYM_PLAS_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_PLAS_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_SLP_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.name: Undesired Results
SYM_SLP_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.description: Unfavourable outcomes are part and parcel of performing surgeries of any kind. Post-treatment undesired results occur after a surgery or treatment has been completed and the result differs from what was expected. This may lead to further complications and increased stress for the patient.

SYM_SLP_S_SCAR_TISSUE_V2.name: S Scar Tissue
SYM_SLP_S_SCAR_TISSUE_V2.description: A scar is a mark that is left on the skin after a wound or an injury to the surface of the skin has healed. The formation of scars or scar tissue is a normal part of the healing process, and occurs when the skin is cut due to injury or surgery. The growth of scar tissue is influenced by many factors, these include: The severity of the original injury. The size, depth and location of the wound on the body. Excess scar tissue, layers deep, can significantly reduce function and movement months after surgery. This can have negative effects and decrease patient satisfaction.

SYM_SLP_PLAS_NERVE_DAMAGE_V2.name: Plas Nerve Damage
SYM_SLP_PLAS_NERVE_DAMAGE_V2.description: Postoperative neuropathic pain (PONP) can develop if nerves were damaged during a surgery. Damaged nerves cannot correctly transmit signals from various parts of the body to the brain. Instead, these signals become exaggerated, causing chronic pain that may persist for months or even years.

SYM_SLP_B_CAPSULAR_V2.name: B Capsular
SYM_SLP_B_CAPSULAR_V2.description: Capsular contracture is a breast augmentation complication that develops when internal scar tissue forms a tight or constricting capsule around a breast implant, contracting it until it becomes misshapen and hard.

SYM_SLP_BARSA_INFECTION_V2.name: Barsa Infection
SYM_SLP_BARSA_INFECTION_V2.description: At face value, bacterial infection is a problem for any implantable medical device, including breast implants. Symptoms of an infection can include a high fever and breast pain, redness and/or swelling. If it appears that antibiotics alone won't clear the infection, then the surgeon may need to clean out the infected tissue and either replace or completely remove the implant.

SYM_SLP_B_IMPLANT_EXTRUSION_V2.name: B Implant Extrusion
SYM_SLP_B_IMPLANT_EXTRUSION_V2.description: In relatively rare cases after implant reconstruction surgery, the surface of the implant or tissue expander comes through the skin and becomes exposed. This is called “extrusion". The patient may develop an infection if they have an extruded or exposed implant. If the breast implant has already extruded or is about to extrude, the plastic surgeon will have to remove it or replace it via corrective surgery.

SYM_SLP_NOSE_SEPTALPERF_V2.name: Nose Septalperf
SYM_SLP_NOSE_SEPTALPERF_V2.description: A nasal septum perforation is a medical condition in which the nasal septum, the bony/cartilaginous wall dividing the nasal cavities, develops a hole or fissure. Some perforations can cause bleeding, pain, and a whistling sound when inhaling. If dried blood or scabs build up, you could also have trouble breathing through your nose. Many perforations do not need to be closed but serious or complicated ones can lead to infections.

SYM_SLP_EYE_GLOBE_PERFORATION_V2.name: Eye Globe Perforation
SYM_SLP_EYE_GLOBE_PERFORATION_V2.description: Globe rupture occurs when the integrity of the outer membranes of the eye is disrupted by blunt or penetrating trauma. Any full-thickness injury to the cornea, sclera, or both is considered an open globe injury and is approached in the same manner in the acute setting. Globe rupture is an ophthalmologic emergency and prompt action is critical to maximizing functional outcome. Although the globe's position within the orbit protects it from injury in many situations, damage to the posterior segment of the eye is associated with a very high frequency of permanent visual loss.

SYM_SLP_EYE_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.name: Eye Undesired Results
SYM_SLP_EYE_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.description: Unsatisfactory eye alignment after surgery is the most common complication when correcting Strabismus and other eye misalignment issues. Despite careful pre-operative measurements and utilization of common surgical dosage tables, a certain percentage of patients will be overcorrected or undercorrected after surgery requiring corrective surgical procedures to alleviate the issue.

SYM_SLP_EYE_CIC_V2.name: Eye Cic
SYM_SLP_EYE_CIC_V2.description: Conjunctival inclusion cysts occur when conjunctival epithelial cells are buried beneath the conjunctival surface during surgery. These cells can multiply over time to create a subconjunctival cyst days to years after the original surgery. Conjunctival scaring is more common after a re-operation and also after a surgical resection, when advancement of the muscle belly may advance the thicker posterior Tenon’s capsule closer to the limbus where it is more visible.

SYM_SLP_EYE_SLIPPED_MUSCLE_V2.name: Eye Slipped Muscle
SYM_SLP_EYE_SLIPPED_MUSCLE_V2.description: Slipped muscles develop when the muscular capsule is imbricated without including the muscle or muscle tendon during strabismus surgery. When the capsule is reattached to the sclera, the tendon and muscle are then free to slip posteriorally from the site of attachment.

SYM_CRP_NASAL_HEMATOMA.name: Nasal Hematoma
SYM_CRP_NASAL_HEMATOMA.description: A nasal septal hematoma is a collection of blood within the septum of the nose. The septum is the part of the nose between the nostrils. An injury disrupts the blood vessels so that fluid and blood may collect under the lining.

SYM_CRP_BLEPHAROPTOSIS.name: Blepharoptosis
SYM_CRP_BLEPHAROPTOSIS.description: Blepharoptosis, also known as ptosis, is a drooping of the upper eyelid that may affect one or both eyes. The eyelid may droop only slightly or may droop enough to cover the pupil and block vision.

SYM_SLP_IMPLANT_INFECTION01.name: Cheek Implant Infection
SYM_SLP_IMPLANT_INFECTION01.description: The patient has developed an infection around a recent cheek implant. Such an infection is serious and the implant will need to be removed and the wound cleaned.

SYM_SLP_FAILED_CA01.name: Failed Cheek Augmentation
SYM_SLP_FAILED_CA01.description: Due to surgery complications, the patient has been unable to have a successful cosmetic surgery outcome. They will not be happy about this.

SYM_CRP_CSFRHINORRHEA.name: Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Rhinorrhea
SYM_CRP_CSFRHINORRHEA.description: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) rhinorrhea is a condition where the fluid that surrounds the brain leaks into the nose and sinuses. Head trauma, surgery, or even birth defects can make a hole in the membranes that hold this fluid. It then leaks into the nose or ear, causing a watery, runny nose.

SYM_CRP_LOW_NOSEBRIDGE.name: Very Low Nose Bridge
SYM_CRP_LOW_NOSEBRIDGE.description: The patient has an exceptionally low nasal bridge. This can cause issues concerning the functionality of the nose.

SYM_CRP_DISPROPORT_APPEAR.name: Disproportionate Appearance (Nose)
SYM_CRP_DISPROPORT_APPEAR.description: The appearance and size of the patient's nose is noticeably difference from what would be considered normal.

SYM_CRP_PINCHED_NOSTRILS.name: Pinched Nostrils
SYM_CRP_PINCHED_NOSTRILS.description: A pinched nasal tip refers to a nose that is excessively narrowed in the region of the tip. This type of nasal abnormality can arise as a congenital problem, meaning the patient was born with this or as a result of having had prior unsuccessful rhinoplasty surgery.

SYM_CRP_NOSETOOHIGH.name: High Tip Of The Nose
SYM_CRP_NOSETOOHIGH.description: The patient's nose is pointed unusually high at the tip.

SYM_CRP_UNEVEN_NOSTRILS.name: Uneven Nostrils
SYM_CRP_UNEVEN_NOSTRILS.description: One of the patient's nostrils is of a noticeably different size compared to the other.

SYM_CRP_OCULAR_EXTRUDE.name: Ocular Contents Extruding From Globe
SYM_CRP_OCULAR_EXTRUDE.description: The patient's eye is extruding the internal contents of the globe of the eye.

SYM_CRP_OCULAR_MOTILITY.name: Loss Of Ocular Motility
SYM_CRP_OCULAR_MOTILITY.description: Loss of Ocular Motility, also know as Oculomotor Dysfunction, occurs when there is the absence or defect of controlled, voluntary, and purposeful eye movement.

SYM_CRP_TISSUE_SHRAPNEL.name: Tissue Shrapnel Damage
SYM_CRP_TISSUE_SHRAPNEL.description: The patient has shallow tissue damage from shrapnel.

SYM_CRP_FACE_HEMATOMA.name: Facial Hematoma
SYM_CRP_FACE_HEMATOMA.description: A facial hematoma is a collection of blood within the face. An injury disrupts the blood vessels so that fluid and blood may collect under the lining.

SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_UMBILICUS.name: Loss Of Umbilicus
SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_UMBILICUS.description: Loss of the umbilicus or naval results in the loss of a person's belly button. While this will cause no health issues it might make a person embarrassed especially if it was a complication of abdominal surgery.

SYM_CRP_BURNING_SENSE.name: Burning Sensation
SYM_CRP_BURNING_SENSE.description: The patient is experiencing a painful burning sensation.

SYM_CRP_SKIN_DISCOLOURATION.name: Skin Discolouration
SYM_CRP_SKIN_DISCOLOURATION.description: The patient has patches of skin that noticeably differ from their normal skin tone.

SYM_CRP_ULCERATION.name: Ulceration
SYM_CRP_ULCERATION.description: Ulceration is the development or formation of an ulcer on a part of the body. Following ulceration, the affected area may bleed or produce unpleasant discharges. Ulceration on the skin can cause a painful sore that is prone to infection and the formation of pus.

SYM_CRP_CVI.name: Chronic Venous Insufficiency
SYM_CRP_CVI.description: Chronic venous insufficiency occurs when the leg veins don't allow blood to flow back up to the heart. Normally, the valves in veins make sure that blood flows toward the heart. But when these valves don't work well, blood can also flow backwards. This can cause blood to collect (pool) in the legs.

SYM_CRP_ECCHYMOSIS.name: Ecchymosis
SYM_CRP_ECCHYMOSIS.description: Ecchymosis is a skin discoloration that results from bleeding underneath the skin. Unlike a bruise, Ecchymosis isn't caused by a blow or impact that ruptured blood vessels.

SYM_CRP_SF_THROMBOPHLEBITIS.name: Superficial Thrombophlebitis
SYM_CRP_SF_THROMBOPHLEBITIS.description: Superficial thrombophlebitis (STP) refers to inflammation and clot formation in the superficial vein which runs under the skin. It commonly occurs in the varicose veins of the leg, but it can also occur in any superficial vein after an injury.

SYM_CRP_SKINBURN_MILD.name: First Degree Burn
SYM_CRP_SKINBURN_MILD.description: A first-degree burn is also called a superficial burn or wound. It's an injury that affects the first layer of the skin.

SYM_CRP_EDEMA.name: Edema
SYM_CRP_EDEMA.description: The patient has swollen body parts from an excess of watery fluid collecting in their tissue.

SYM_PLAS_C001_V2.name: C001
SYM_PLAS_C001_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Cosmetic Breast Augmentation to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C002_V2.name: C002
SYM_PLAS_C002_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want Liposuction to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C003_V2.name: C003
SYM_PLAS_C003_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want Augmentation Rhinoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C004_V2.name: C004
SYM_PLAS_C004_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Hair Transplant to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C005.name: Patient Request (Chemical Peel)
SYM_PLAS_C005.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Chemical Peel to enhance their appearance. The patient wishes to only have a light peel, so nothing too taxing on the skin or requiring long term treatment.

SYM_PLAS_C006.name: Patient Request (Spider Veins)
SYM_PLAS_C006.description: The patient has expressed that they want a procedure to remove spider veins (Telangiectasias) from a part or parts of their body to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C007.name: Patient Request (Skin Conditioning)
SYM_PLAS_C007.description: The patient has expressed that they want botox injections to help condition their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C008.name: Patient Request (Skin Conditioning)
SYM_PLAS_C008.description: The patient has expressed that they want dermal filler injections to help condition their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C009.name: Patient Request (Skin Rejuvenation)
SYM_PLAS_C009.description: The patient has expressed that they want a laser resurfacing procedure to help rejuvenation their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R001.name: Blepharospam (Evaluation)
SYM_PLAS_R001.description: There is no particular test for blepharospasm. Diagnosis relies on physical examination, medical history and ruling out other possible causes. The doctor has evaluated the patient's symptoms and determined the diagnosis is indeed blepharospasm.

SYM_PLAS_R002_V2.name: R002
SYM_PLAS_R002_V2.description: The patient has been confirmed to have misaligned eyes.

SYM_PLAS_R003.name: Simple Varicose Veins
SYM_PLAS_R003.description: The patient has varicose veins but fortunately they appear to be rather simple and lack the signs or symptoms of a more serious diagnosis. Varicose veins that cause symptoms should not be ignored and treated promptly to prevent patient discomfort and to help prevent future issues.

SYM_PLAS_R004_M.name: R004
SYM_PLAS_R004_M.description: The patient's finger is permanently bent inwards towards the palm. The bending is a result of thick tissue forcing the finger into a contractured position. Evaluation suggests this is the cause of a Dupuytren’s Contracture.

SYM_PLAS_R004_F.name: R004
SYM_PLAS_R004_F.description: The patient's finger is permanently bent inwards towards the palm. The bending is a result of thick tissue forcing the finger into a contractured position. Evaluation suggests this is the cause of a Dupuytren’s Contracture.

SYM_PLAS_R005.name: Hypertrophic Scarring
SYM_PLAS_R005.description: The patient has presented with Hypertrophic Scarring that is giving them some degree of discomfort. While these scars aren’t dangerous or life-threatening correcting the discomfort will help enhance the patient's quality of life.

SYM_PLAS_R006.name: Keloid Scarring
SYM_PLAS_R006.description: The patient has presented with Keloid Scarring that is giving them some degree of discomfort. While these scars aren’t dangerous or life-threatening correcting the discomfort will help enhance the patient's quality of life.

SYM_PLAS_RT001_V2.name: Rt001
SYM_PLAS_RT001_V2.description: One of the patient's digits has been amputated due to an accident or injury.

SYM_PLAS_C010.name: Patient Request (Mastopexy)
SYM_PLAS_C010.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Mastopexy to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C011.name: Patient Request (Septoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_C011.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Septoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R008.name: Nasal Deformities
SYM_PLAS_R008.description: The patient has nasal deformities that effect the aesthetics and functions of the nose.

SYM_PLAS_RT002.name: Amputated Toe
SYM_PLAS_RT002.description: One of the patient's toes has been amputated due to an accident or injury.

SYM_PLAS_RT003.name: Irreparable Ocular Trauma
SYM_PLAS_RT003.description: One of the patient's eyes has been all but obliterated and will not be able to be recovered.

SYM_PLAS_CKU001.name: Checkup (Breast Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_CKU001.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Breast Augmentation surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU002.name: Checkup (Augmentation Rhinoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU002.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Augmentation Rhinoplasty surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU003.name: Checkup (Liposuction)
SYM_PLAS_CKU003.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Liposuction surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU004.name: Checkup (Mastopexy)
SYM_PLAS_CKU004.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Mastopexy surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU005.name: Checkup (Septoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU005.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Septoplasty surgery.

SYM_PLAS_C012.name: Patient Request (Cheek Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_C012.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Cheek Augmentation to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C013.name: Patient Request (Abdominoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_C013.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Abdominoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R010.name: Venous Ulceration
SYM_PLAS_R010.description: The patient has an abnormal wound caused by damaged veins.

SYM_PLAS_CKU006.name: Checkup (Cheek Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_CKU006.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Cheek Augmentation surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU007.name: Checkup (Abdominoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU007.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Abdominoplasty surgery.

SYM_PLAS_R009_V2.name: Complex Varicose Veins
SYM_PLAS_R009_V2.description: The patient has varicose veins and unfortunately they appear to be rather complex and have the signs or symptoms of a more serious diagnosis. Varicose veins that cause symptoms should not be ignored and treated promptly to prevent patient discomfort and to help prevent future issues.

EXM_CRP_ELECTROPHORESIS.name: Electrophoresis Sampling
EXM_CRP_ELECTROPHORESIS.description: Electrophoresis is a laboratory technique used to separate DNA, RNA, or protein molecules based on their size and electrical charge. An electric current is used to move molecules to be separated through a gel. Pores in the gel work like a sieve, allowing smaller molecules to move faster than larger molecules. The conditions used during electrophoresis can be adjusted to separate molecules in a desired size range. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_ELECTROPHORESIS_TEST.name: Electrophoresis Testing
EXM_CRP_ELECTROPHORESIS_TEST.description: Electrophoresis is a laboratory technique used to separate DNA, RNA, or protein molecules based on their size and electrical charge. An electric current is used to move molecules to be separated through a gel. Pores in the gel work like a sieve, allowing smaller molecules to move faster than larger molecules. The conditions used during electrophoresis can be adjusted to separate molecules in a desired size range. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

TRT_CRP_COMPRESSION_GARMENT.name: Compression Garment
TRT_CRP_COMPRESSION_GARMENT.description: Wearing a compression garment after liposuction helps the body recover and increases the patients comfort during the healing process. A compression garment helps to correct overtime, baggy or irregular skin surface features caused by the surgery. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_BOTOX.name: Botulinum Toxin
TRT_CRP_BOTOX.description: Botulinum toxin (Botox) is a neurotoxic protein produced by the bacterium Clostridium botulinum and related species. It prevents the release of the neurotransmitter acetylcholine from axon endings at the neuromuscular junction and thus causes flaccid paralysis. While useful for various Plastic Surgery procedures, Botulinum toxin is the most poisonous substance known. Intoxication can occur naturally as a result of either wound or intestinal infection or by ingesting preformed toxin in food. The estimated human lethal dose of type A toxin is 1.3–2.1 ng/kg intravenously or intramuscularly, 10–13 ng/kg when inhaled, or 1000 ng/kg when taken by mouth.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_ATROPINE_EYEDROP.name: Atropine Eye Drops
TRT_CRP_ATROPINE_EYEDROP.description: Atropine Eye Drops belongs to a class of medicines called antimuscarinic agents. It is used to dilate (enlarge) the pupil of the eye and to temporarily paralyse the lens so that your doctor can examine your eye(s). Putting cycloplegic drugs, such as atropine sulphate, into the healthy eye causes blurred vision and loss of ability to focus on different distances, forcing the lazy eye to compensate.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CHEMPEEL_L.name: Alpha Hydroxy Acid Peel
TRT_CRP_CHEMPEEL_L.description: Alpha Hydroxy Acids (AHAs) are usually derived from citric (citrus fruits), glycolic (sugar cane), lactic (fermented milk), malic (apples) and tartaric (grapes). Generally, glycolic and lactic acid are the two most commonly applied. AHAs are the mildest of all chemical peels. They are primarily beneficial for the treatment of very fine lines, dry and dehydrated skin, mild skin and pigment irregularities and acne. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPY.name: Sclerotherapy
TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPY.description: Sclerotherapy is a treatment that involves the injection of a chemical solution into blood vessels, usually spider veins or varicose veins on the legs. Sclerotherapy irritates the vessel walls, making them stick together. This leads to fibrosis, or scarring, of the blood vessel. The unwanted veins injected with a sclerosing solution then dissolve over a period of weeks as the body naturally absorbs the treated vein. Sclerotherapy is a non-invasive procedure taking only about 10 minutes to perform. The downtime is minimal, in comparison to an invasive varicose vein surgery. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SKIN_LT.name: Skin Laser Therapy
TRT_CRP_SKIN_LT.description: Laser Therapy or Laser Resurfacing is a procedure that uses a laser to improve the appearance of skin or treat minor facial flaws by removing layers of skin. The two most common types of resurfacing lasers are; Carbon dioxide (CO2), this type of laser is used to treat wrinkles, scars, warts and other conditions. The second type is Erbium, this type of laser is used to remove superficial and moderately deep lines and wrinkles on the face, hands, neck and chest. It causes fewer side effects than CO2 lasers.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CHE_INJECT.name: Enzyme Injections (CCH)
TRT_CRP_CHE_INJECT.description: Injecting a type of enzyme, normally collagenase extracted from C histolyticum, into the taut cord of the patient's palm can soften and weaken it allowing the doctor to manipulate the hand in an attempt to break the cord and straighten the fingers. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS2.name: Corticosteroid Injections
TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS2.description: Corticosteroids (also known as steroids) are medicines that can be used to treat inflammation. When corticosteroids are injected into or around a painful area (such as a joint or muscle) they can reduce the inflammation in that area, relieving pain, reducing tissue swelling, and improving function and mobility. A Doctor may inject a corticosteroid solution directly into a hypertrophic scar or keloid, which may help reduce its size. Steroids break the bonds between collagen fibers, which reduces the amount of scar tissue beneath the skin.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CRYOSURGERY.name: Cryosurgery
TRT_CRP_CRYOSURGERY.description: Cryosurgery is a type of surgery that involves the use of extreme cold to destroy abnormal tissues, such as tumors. The surgery most often involves the use of liquid nitrogen, although carbon dioxide and argon may also be used. Cryosurgery works by taking advantage of the destructive force of freezing temperatures on cells. When their temperature sinks beyond a certain level ice crystals begin forming inside the cells and, because of their lower density, eventually tear apart those cells. Further harm to malignant growth will result once the blood vessels supplying the affected tissue begin to freeze.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_HYAACID_FILL.name: Hyaluronic Acid Filler
TRT_CRP_HYAACID_FILL.description: Injectable hyaluronic acid (HA) is a type of temporary dermal filler. Hyaluronic acid is found naturally through out the body, with the highest concentrations in the joints, eyes, and skin. In the skin hyaluronic acid attracts water, which allows the skin to become more hydrated. Injectable HA is used to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, facial folds, and to create structure, framework, and volume to the face and lips. The effects of injectable hyaluronic acid are seen immediately. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L1.name: Hospitalised Monitoring (Short)
TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L1.description: Watch and wait monitoring of a patient's condition from the hospital. This allows for quicker response to issues and be necessary for high risk complications that arise from symptoms or treatments.

TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L2.name: Hospitalised Monitoring (Moderate)
TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L2.description: Watch and wait monitoring of a patient's condition from the hospital. This allows for quicker response to issues and be necessary for high risk complications that arise from symptoms or treatments.

TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L3.name: Hospitalised Monitoring (Lengthy)
TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L3.description: Watch and wait monitoring of a patient's condition from the hospital. This allows for quicker response to issues and be necessary for high risk complications that arise from symptoms or treatments.

TRT_CRP_SHALLOWDEBRISREMOVAL.name: Shallow Debris Removal
TRT_CRP_SHALLOWDEBRISREMOVAL.description: Removal of shallow debris and foreign objects embedded in the patient's skin or face and disinfecting the effected areas.

TRT_CRP_DIURETICS.name: Diuretics
TRT_CRP_DIURETICS.description: Medications that lower the amount of sodium and water in the body. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology

SUR_SLP_AUGMENTATION_MAMMOPLASTY.name: Augmentation Mammoplasty
SUR_SLP_AUGMENTATION_MAMMOPLASTY.description: Breast augmentation is a cosmetic surgery technique using breast-implants and fat-graft mammoplasty techniques to increase the size, change the shape, and alter the texture of the breasts of a woman. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_CORRECTIVE_SURGERY.name: Corrective Surgery
SUR_SLP_CORRECTIVE_SURGERY.description: It is not uncommon for patients to occasionally require corrective surgery, this is carried out to address concerns or complications relating to a previous surgery. Addressing these concerns will help greatly with patient satisfaction at the expense of additional surgery time and further complication risks. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_SAL.name: Suction Assisted Lipectomy (SAL)
SUR_SLP_SAL.description: Liposuction is invasive surgery that aims to improve the body’s contours and proportion by removing localised and disproportional deposits of fat. Liposuction can remove up to 5 litres of fat with relative safety during one session. Suction Assisted Lipectomy (SAL) is where fat is removed from the body using a vacuum suction. SAL may be dry, wet or tumescent (Tumescent liposuction) depending on the volume of saline (salt solution) which is injected into the operation site before the procedure begins. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_RHINOPLASTY.name: Rhinoplasty
SUR_SLP_RHINOPLASTY.description: Rhinoplasty, commonly known as a nose job, is a plastic surgery procedure for correcting and reconstructing the nose. There are two types of plastic surgery used – reconstructive surgery that restores the form and functions of the nose and cosmetic surgery that improves the appearance of the nose.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_HAIRT.name: Follicular Unit Transplantation
SUR_SLP_HAIRT.description: Follicular unit extraction, sometimes referred to simply as FUE, is a method of hair transplant surgery that manually removes individual hair follicles from the patient's scalp. Once extracted, hairs are then implanted to bald or thinning areas around the head.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_MASTOPEXY.name: Mastopexy
SUR_SLP_MASTOPEXY.description: Mastopexy, or breast lift as it is commonly known, is a Cosmetic Surgery procedure designed to lift and re-contour sagging breasts or breasts that have lost volume. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_SEPTOPLASTY.name: Septoplasty
SUR_SLP_SEPTOPLASTY.description: Septoplasty, or alternatively submucous septal resection and septal reconstruction, is a corrective surgical procedure done to straighten a deviated nasal septum – the nasal septum being the partition between the two nasal cavities. Ideally, the septum should run down the centre of the nose. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_CHEEK_AUGMENTATION.name: Cheek Augmentation
SUR_SLP_CHEEK_AUGMENTATION.description: Cheek augmentation surgery, also known as cheek implant surgery is a procedure where your plastic surgeon inserts a solid implant over your cheekbone which enhances your facial contour by making your cheeks look fuller providing an overall better definition to your face. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_ABDOMINOPLASTY.name: Abdominoplasty
SUR_SLP_ABDOMINOPLASTY.description: Abdominoplasty or "tummy tuck" is a cosmetic surgery procedure used to make the abdomen thinner and more firm. The surgery involves the removal of excess skin and fat from the middle and lower abdomen in order to tighten the muscle and fascia of the abdominal wall.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_SCAR_REVISION_SURGERY.name: Scar Revision Surgery
SUR_SLP_PLAS_SCAR_REVISION_SURGERY.description: Scar revision is surgery to improve or reduce the appearance of scars. It also restores function, and corrects skin changes (disfigurement) caused by an injury, wound, poor healing, or previous surgery. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL.name: Breast Implant Removal
SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL.description: Breast implant infections are usually caused by Staphylococcus aureus and coagulase-negative staphylococci. Gram-negative bacilli are rarely reported to be involved in breast implant infections. Although rare, infections of this type generally require the implants to be removed. This surgery removes the breast implants and cleans the tissue around the implant site. Follow-up anti-biotic courses are normally required. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_CAPSULECTOMY.name: Capsulectomy
SUR_SLP_PLAS_CAPSULECTOMY.description: Capsulectomy is the surgical removal of scar tissue or capsule that has become thickened and hardened around a breast implant. The capsule is composed of fibroblasts, collagen, and blood vessels. Performing a capsulectomy should alleviate the discomfort and improve the appearance and feel of the patients breasts. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_EYEMSURGERY.name: Eye Muscle Surgery
SUR_SLP_PLAS_EYEMSURGERY.description: Eye muscle surgery is a surgery to correct strabismus (eye misalignment) or nystagmus (eye wiggling). The surgery involves moving one or more of the eye muscles to adjust the position of the eye or eyes.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_EMERG_REPAIR.name: Emergency Surgery (Plastic)
SUR_SLP_PLAS_EMERG_REPAIR.description:
Emergency Surgery can be defined as surgery that is required to deal with an acute threat to life, organ, limb or tissue caused by external trauma, acute disease process, acute exacerbation of a chronic disease process, or complication of a surgical or other interventional procedure. 

Importance: This surgery is considered critical and should be a priority. 

Required room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_REPLANT.name: Replantation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_REPLANT.description: Replantation refers to the surgical reattachment of a body part (such as a finger, hand, or toe) that has been completely cut from the body. The goal of replantation surgery is to reattach and restore function to as much of the injured part as possible.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENUCLEATION.name: Enucleation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENUCLEATION.description:
Enucleation is the removal of the eye that leaves the eye muscles and remaining orbital contents intact. This type of ocular surgery is indicated for a number of ocular tumors, in eyes that have suffered severe trauma, and in eyes that are otherwise blind and painful. A spherical implant (artificial eyeball) is used to replace the removed eyeball. 

Importance: This surgery is considered critical and should be a priority. 

Required room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL_01.name: Cheek Implant Infection
SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL_01.description: Cheek implant infections are usually caused by Staphylococcus aureus and coagulase-negative staphylococci. Although rare, infections of this type generally require the implants to be removed. This surgery removes the cheek implants and cleans the tissue around the implant site. Follow-up anti-biotic courses are normally required. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENDOVENOUS_LASER_AB.name: Endovenous Laser Ablation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENDOVENOUS_LASER_AB.description: Endovenous laser ablation, also known as endovenous laser therapy, is a procedure that uses heat from a laser to reduce varicose veins.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_SURGICAL_DEBRIDEMENT.name: Surgical Debridement
SUR_SLP_PLAS_SURGICAL_DEBRIDEMENT.description: Debridement is the medical removal of dead, damaged, or infected tissue to improve the healing potential of the remaining healthy tissue. Surgical or "sharp" debridement and laser debridement under anesthesia are the fastest methods of debridement. They are very selective, meaning that the person performing the debridement has complete control over which tissue is removed and which is left behind. Surgical debridement can be performed in the operating room or bedside, depending on the extent of the necrotic material and a patient's ability to tolerate the procedure. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.
